methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Scout AI is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Colby Adcock and Collin Otis that has raised $15M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public companies operating in the same sector, including peers such as Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI at similar or adjacent stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $15M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $38M. The peer-median approach examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed)—yielding a median valuation of $225M across a $60M–$500M range.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $32M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $131M. The wide spread reflects meaningful uncertainty inherent in valuing early-stage defense technology. Key drivers of valuation placement within this range include product validation, customer traction, competitive positioning relative to the peer set, and trajectory toward Series A fundraising. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median results suggests Scout AI's stage-adjusted comparables command substantially higher valuations, warranting closer examination of technology differentiation and market opportunity relative to peers.
